a77A8C1CD-21F5-F018-F44C-9259060E2394.title: Presentation of Data Modeling for Power BI

a64AAD278-E449-ADAD-7943-69FA8464A714.title: How to download and complete exercises
a64AAD278-E449-ADAD-7943-69FA8464A714.text:
In this section, you can download all the video course exercises, demos, and slides.

You can use the slides to review the training content. Keep in mind the book Analyzing Data with Power BI and Power Pivot for Excel presents a complete written explanation of the topics described in the videos - you might be interested in the book for an equivalent content in written form.

The demos are files we use in videos to illustrate how to correctly build a data model. You need Power BI Desktop to replicate the same demos on your PC.

The exercises are described in a document illustrating an activity to complete. You should try to execute the task requested of you after each lesson. You have files describing an initial situation, and a number of steps required to obtain the proposed solution(s).

After completing the task requested, you should compare it with the proposed solution. You might have obtained a different result, so it is important to compare your result with the one we proposed. You want to ensure you understand what the different approaches are.

a7A48CFAF-040B-E6E3-AE6A-E01F7819561E.title: Exercises download
a7A48CFAF-040B-E6E3-AE6A-E01F7819561E.text:
This ZIP file contains all the files required for the exercises of the video course.
In the root folder, you will find the PDF file with the description of all the exercises.

aF0307A1A-CA9F-38A1-36CA-4124DA2F8DC7.title: Demos download
aF0307A1A-CA9F-38A1-36CA-4124DA2F8DC7.text: This ZIP file contains all the files used in the demos of the video course.

a4B43E5FA-AC07-F4B7-8A5C-1AF5F2A79E43.title: Slides of the video course
a4B43E5FA-AC07-F4B7-8A5C-1AF5F2A79E43.text: This file contains all the slides used in the video course.

aDB460AC0-837D-B70A-05C1-D805523E1CCF.title: SQL Server Contoso DW database
aDB460AC0-837D-B70A-05C1-D805523E1CCF.text:
This ZIP file contains the Contoso DW.bak file, which is a SQL Server backup that you can restore on Microsoft SQL Server.

This is the data source used for exercises and most of the demos. It is not necessary to complete the exercises and to analyze the demo files.

However, if you are interested in opening the Query Editor, you have to restore this database on Microsoft SQL Server and you have to correct the data source of the PBIX file so that it points to your instance of SQL Server.

files.title: Contoso SQL Server.zip
files.url: https://mysqlbi.blob.core.windows.net/datamodeling/Data Modeling for Power BI 2018 Contoso SQL Server.zip?sv=2016-05-31&se=2023-06-14T13%3A08%3A14Z&sr=b&sp=r&spr=https&sig=qPO6P1vX7HdjrcoCOrCFG8I6nMPj0yl1JLS%2F4ra5L8g%3D

a52F00B4F-CA1D-765C-E664-3C53BD2B4AB4.title: Introduction to data modeling
a52F00B4F-CA1D-765C-E664-3C53BD2B4AB4.text:
In this section, you learn the basic principles of data modeling. Through a set of examples, we slowly introduce important concepts like relationships, granularity, and the difference between a fact table and a dimension.

We also introduce the concept of a star schema and how important it is in the design of any analytical data model.

This section is very important to set a common theoretical background that will guide you through all the following chapters.

a1ED9B95A-5779-B24C-1C2C-1329D1748FBC.title: If you don't have a star schema

aDA228757-3F2B-943E-D113-99F7ACB59044.title: LAB number 1
aDA228757-3F2B-943E-D113-99F7ACB59044.text:
Basic data modeling

Time to practice and work on some models.

In this lab you start do distinguish between dimensions and values, you discover where to store information to obtain the desired result and move the first steps in the world of data modeling.

Refer to lab number 1 on the hands-on manual.

a582E76ED-280D-DA7F-8AD1-7E980045158F.title: Header / detail tables
a582E76ED-280D-DA7F-8AD1-7E980045158F.text:
Several data models include tables forming a common pattern called "header / detail". Such data models are not based on star schemas and they are not the ideal structure for analytical purposes.

In this section, you learn how to correctly transform a header / detail into a star schema, taking care of the allocation of numeric values.

aA82548A2-A5F5-1831-4D68-6428F19E7B8F.title: Bidirectional filtering is not the way to go

aCB3E1285-EA31-D76E-A9D1-3B2F166AB884.title: LAB number 2
aCB3E1285-EA31-D76E-A9D1-3B2F166AB884.text:
Header / Detail

Time to practice and work on some models.

You will need to solve the header/detail pattern by yourself, following the instructions on the exercise manual.

Once the scenario is solved, you will need to go a few steps further, of course!

Refer to lab number 2 on the hands-on manual.

aC464BF03-6FCD-24AD-84BF-3C31D160D803.title: Multiple fact tables
aC464BF03-6FCD-24AD-84BF-3C31D160D803.text:
If you have more than one fact table in a data model, you need a correct set of dimensions and relationships to properly slice and dice values.

In this section, you learn how to setup a data model with multiple fact tables. We also introduce the concept of model ambiguity and how to manage models that appear to include ambiguous relationships.

a7B80FD7C-327A-8136-B6FD-69F21FA37605.title: How to properly use multiple fact tables, if present

a833A7B97-B8D3-258C-CE51-254B80E2B298.title: LAB number 3
a833A7B97-B8D3-258C-CE51-254B80E2B298.text:
Multiple fact tables

Time to practice and work on some models.

In these labs you work with multiple fact tables. You will prepare proper dimensions and check some details about the correctness of data.

Refer to lab number 3 on the hands-on manual.

a68719DD1-D2EE-85B3-46AF-DD9A5D170E03.title: Working with date and time
a68719DD1-D2EE-85B3-46AF-DD9A5D170E03.text:
Date is an important dimension because it is present in the vast majority of data models.

In the first part of this section, you learn how to build a correct date dimension. Later on, we discuss the possible approaches in managing multiple dates. Other topics include calculations over working days and the definition of time periods like Easter holidays, Black Friday sales, or other particular time periods.

a3565FF10-C181-307A-21E1-67DCB00914D3.title: Date attributes in the fact table

aE8488757-1B75-480F-95E6-D656D35ADFDB.title: The model with a date dimension

a1ABD9F70-D2D0-96A0-EA6F-D26D8CA514A8.title: Automatic date grouping in Excel and Power BI

aCB96713E-2943-6FC8-75C2-7B81EB79D996.title: Automatic date grouping in Power BI

a5EF4072F-D36B-3BE6-D70A-549323D57568.title: Quick calculations in Power BI Desktop

aCA9150F0-2D08-8252-1AD3-A2DE791CEC80.title: Multiple date tables with multiple fact tables

a1295A00D-C3CF-57F8-91FF-286788B3465E.title: Weekends are not always the same

a21D4E96B-9ABA-EB12-2A23-CB76055A6858.title: Handling special periods of the year

a547A36A4-738B-DD72-CD61-CA787F0741FF.title: LAB number 4
a547A36A4-738B-DD72-CD61-CA787F0741FF.text:
Working with date and time

Time to practice and work on some models.

You will create a proper date table to browse purchases and sales, and then you will modify a model to analyze events by time.

Refer to lab number 4 on the hands-on manual.

aBBE6CFA3-D63D-0376-EC26-E2B9A7273A91.title: Tracking historical attributes
aBBE6CFA3-D63D-0376-EC26-E2B9A7273A91.text:
A dimension with attributes that never change over time is easy to manage. However, in the real world there could be attributes of entities (like customer or product) whose values change on a daily, monthly or yearly basis.

In this section, you learn how to handle attributes that change over time at different frequencies.

aF2523F41-B25B-2BAE-C8F4-CA64D7D0F633.title: Using snapshots
aF2523F41-B25B-2BAE-C8F4-CA64D7D0F633.text:
A fact table might include numeric values that cannot easily be aggregated over time, such as currency exchange rates and inventory product availability. These fact tables are called snapshots and require special handling, both in terms of data modeling and in terms of DAX calculations.

In this section, you learn different approaches in creating and managing snapshot tables.

a28B92000-0E52-CF1B-4169-60287472CA09.title: Sales versus inventory in the same model

a81331C6E-7136-4AF4-BBF0-3AB8CA6B695B.title: LAB number 5
a81331C6E-7136-4AF4-BBF0-3AB8CA6B695B.text:
Using snapshots

Time to practice and work on some models.

In this lab you build a snapshot at different granularities and experience how the calculations are affected by the snapshot grain.

Refer to lab number 5 on the hands-on manual.

a97590EF3-69EC-E074-BC9A-0810E2B57D4B.title: Analyzing date and time intervals
a97590EF3-69EC-E074-BC9A-0810E2B57D4B.text:
Whenever a fact has a duration, like a subscription that has a start date and an end date, several analyses require calculations that are not trivial to implement with a star schema. For example, a simple calculation like the number of active subscriptions in a certain month can prove to be really challenging if you have not built the right data model.

In this section, you learn several data modeling techniques useful to manage fact tables representing intervals over date and time.

aEE95C74B-1145-F7C6-3721-087165AE02B3.title: Open orders is a snapshot table

a84D5EF83-2AB2-075C-FEB9-2F546839AC5D.title: LAB number 6
a84D5EF83-2AB2-075C-FEB9-2F546839AC5D.text:
Analyzing date and time intervals

Time to practice and work on some models.

In this lab you practice with the shift example, moving from a very simple model to a much more powerful one.

Refer to lab number 6 on the hands-on manual.

a56341756-8FEE-9440-02D3-6BAAEE097328.title: Many-to-many relationships
a56341756-8FEE-9440-02D3-6BAAEE097328.text:
A relationship between two entities might require a many-to-many pattern. For example - one individual plays two sports like tennis and golf, and another plays three sports: soccer, biking and tennis. Each sport is being played by more individuals and each individual plays one or more sports.

Power BI does not handle such scenarios natively, because in Power BI a relationship must be one-to-many or many-to-one. In this section, you learn how to implement many-to-many relationships in a data model for Power BI.

aD31E68CB-42A1-2A0D-8468-19BEB500F93A.title: Additional material
aD31E68CB-42A1-2A0D-8468-19BEB500F93A.text:
New types of relationships have been added to Power BI after the recording of this video course. While we are evaluating a larger update of the course by 2024, we suggest to check the following additional content about many-to-many relationships in Power BI:

Relationships in Power BI and Tabular models (article on SQLBI)

Different options to model many-to-many relationships in Power BI and Tabular (article + video on SQLBI)

Many-to-Many Relationships – The Whiteboard #12 - SQLBI

aE50BC958-FD58-123A-82BA-1BC2A8D61E9A.title: LAB number 7
aE50BC958-FD58-123A-82BA-1BC2A8D61E9A.text:
Many-to-many relationships

Time to practice and work on some models.

In this lab you practice with regular many-to-many, cascading many-to-many and different kind of calculations over many-to-many.

Refer to lab number 7 on the hands-on manual.

a47859160-9162-50C9-75B0-3DEC0AB5B50A.title: Working with different granularities
a47859160-9162-50C9-75B0-3DEC0AB5B50A.text:
Every fact table in a data model has a well-defined level of granularity. However, different fact tables within the same data model can have different granularities. For example, in the same data model you might have a Sales fact table recording sales at the day level, and a Budget fact table containing projected sales for each month and year.

In this section, you learn how to create calculations that correctly aggregate values from tables with different granularities. The techniques described involve both updates to the data model and rather complex DAX code.

a1AF31787-74C2-3C55-B634-23E77BC05D76.title: Reduce granularity on all the tables

a8E234F2D-21F7-0AF2-85B4-462A8C2ED523.title: Using DAX to move the filters

a27C773CE-6E9F-FC09-51BC-B0CAA1D2EA20.title: Use the correct column to slice

aAB782558-BAFF-F43A-29D2-D8F267A86F5F.title: LAB number 8
aAB782558-BAFF-F43A-29D2-D8F267A86F5F.text:
Different granularity

Time to practice and work on some models.

In this lab you work with the budget model, with fact tables at different granularity.

Refer to lab number 8 on the hands-on manual.

a380DC6BC-7280-5C29-4E11-95858FF36925.title: Segmentation data models
a380DC6BC-7280-5C29-4E11-95858FF36925.text: In this section, you learn how to create data models to group entities based on a range of values for a measure. The segmentation can be defined statically (for example, a range of price) or dynamically (for example, sales amount over the period selected in a report).

aA40BE20A-557E-D7EB-96CA-5A202E84AA1A.title: Working with multiple currencies
aA40BE20A-557E-D7EB-96CA-5A202E84AA1A.text:
If a data model contains amounts in different currencies, creating a report can be challenging. Conversions between different currencies is a classic business scenario. Nevertheless, it is also one of the more complex problems to solve from a data modeling point of view.

In this section, you learn some of the most common techniques to manage currency conversions. It covers ready-to-use solutions to different requirements and shows you how to apply several concepts described in other sections.
